## 2026-02-18 — Codex dual Stripe runtime split (production vs tester)

- Implemented dual Stripe runtime handling so tester audience can use test Stripe config while regular production users use primary Stripe config.
- Files:
  - `src/lib/env.ts`
  - `src/lib/billing/stripe-runtime.ts`
  - `src/app/(public)/pricing/actions.ts`
  - `src/app/(public)/pricing/success/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/billing/actions.ts`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/billing/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`
  - `src/lib/accelerator/entitlements.ts`
- Changes:
  - Added optional test-mode Stripe env vars:
    - `STRIPE_TEST_SECRET_KEY`
    - `STRIPE_TEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
    - `STRIPE_TEST_ORGANIZATION_PRICE_ID`
    - `STRIPE_TEST_OPERATIONS_SUPPORT_PRICE_ID`
  - Added centralized Stripe runtime resolver for:
    - audience-based checkout selection (tester vs primary)
    - fallback session retrieval across configured Stripe modes
    - webhook verification across multiple webhook secrets
  - Checkout now writes `stripe_mode` metadata for subscription updates/sessions.
  - Billing portal creation now retries across configured Stripe modes (mode hint aware).
  - Webhook route now validates/processes events against whichever configured Stripe runtime matches signature.
  - Accelerator entitlement Stripe sync now checks all configured Stripe runtimes (when enabled).
- Vercel env actions:
  - Seeded production tester-specific env vars from current production Stripe values.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint src/lib/env.ts src/lib/billing/stripe-runtime.ts 'src/app/(public)/pricing/actions.ts' 'src/app/(public)/pricing/success/page.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/billing/actions.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/billing/page.tsx' 'src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts' src/lib/accelerator/entitlements.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅
- Deploy:
  - `https://coach-house-platform.vercel.app` aliased to deployment `coach-house-platform-6uix0zcmn-caleb-hamernicks-projects.vercel.app`.
- Live Stripe follow-up:
  - Stripe CLI live auth completed for account `acct_1QegK8GWpMHCiyVj`.
  - Returned key is restricted (`rk_live`) and does **not** have permissions for required endpoints (`checkout.sessions.create`, `prices.create`), so it cannot be used as the production server key.
  - Production still needs full-access live server key + live webhook secret + live recurring plan prices to complete true real-card checkout.

## 2026-02-20 — Codex scale-readiness hardening pass (enforcement + app-shell slimming + button consistency)

- Files:
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
  - `AGENTS.md`
  - `docs/agent/workflow-quality.md`
  - `docs/agent/code-structure.md`
  - `docs/agent/codex-execution-playbook.md`
  - `docs/prompt-examples.md`
  - `package.json`
  - `scripts/check-performance-budgets.mjs`
  - `scripts/check-structure-conventions.mjs`
  - `src/app/(admin)/layout.tsx`
  - `src/components/app-shell.tsx`
  - `src/components/ui/input-group.tsx`
  - `src/app/(admin)/admin/modules/[id]/_components/module-builder/dnd-list.tsx`
  - `src/components/account-settings/sections/section-helpers.tsx`
  - `src/components/account-settings/account-settings-dialog-shell.tsx`
- Changes:
  - Aligned CI with required repository gates by adding `check:structure` and `test:rls` to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
  - Added consolidated local quality command: `pnpm check:quality`.
  - Tightened structure guardrail from 1200 -> 500 LOC hard cap with an explicit transitional allowlist for known legacy large files.
  - Updated structure/workflow contracts to document stricter decomposition expectations and CI parity.
  - Fixed performance budget route-key drift (`/(dashboard)/community/page`) and switched dashboard/admin limits to realistic transitional ceilings that still enforce regressions.
  - Reduced admin first-load bundle pressure by dynamically splitting heavy app-shell islands and introducing an explicit `admin` shell context:
    - dynamic imports for global search, onboarding surfaces, paywall overlay, tutorial manager, and case-study autofill tools.
    - admin layout now uses `context="admin"` and skips those non-admin overlays.
  - Replaced design-system drifted raw buttons with shared `Button` usage in high-confidence hotspots:
    - `input-group` helper button
    - admin module-builder row drag/delete actions
    - account settings side navigation links
    - account settings mobile header save/cancel controls
  - Added repository-specific execution prompt templates in `docs/agent/codex-execution-playbook.md` and marked `docs/prompt-examples.md` as ideation-only.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm lint` ✅
  - `pnpm check:structure` ✅ (warnings only for allowlisted/near-threshold files)
  - `pnpm test:snapshots` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance` ✅
  - `pnpm test:rls` ✅
  - `pnpm build` ✅
  - `pnpm check:perf` ✅
  - `pnpm check:quality` ✅
- Notes:
  - Raw `<button>` count in active source reduced from 62 -> 56 in this pass while preserving semantic raw-button usage where appropriate.
  - `/admin` first-load budget improved from ~946.7KB to ~872KB after app-shell client-island splitting.

  - Implemented a route-local workspace board feature slice with React Flow card canvas, corrected semantic edge wiring, and size controls (`sm`/`md`/full-page navigation) per card.
  - Added preset-driven auto-layout (`balanced`, `calendar-focused`, `communications-focused`) plus persisted board state in `organizations.profile.workspace_board_v1` via server action.
  - Added temporary collaboration invite workflow (hours/days/months), active invite list, and revoke support, persisted in `organizations.profile.workspace_collaboration_v1` with role-based permission checks.
  - Added live collaboration affordances:
    - realtime cursor overlay via Supabase channel broadcast,
    - presence avatar stack for active collaborators,
    - `CurrentUserAvatar` usage in workspace toolbar.
  - Added workspace-specific cards:
    - organization overview,
    - formation status,
    - brand kit,
    - economic engine (copy update from funding-mix framing),
    - minimal calendar card with quick recurring board meeting action,
    - communications card with channel toggles, morphing post preview, and activity heatmap.
  - Added navigation entry points to workspace from overview and editor surfaces.
  - Added compatibility export (`createClient`) in Supabase browser client module so generated registry hooks compile under repo conventions.
  - Added an implementation contract document for future sessions covering route integration, file tree, permissions, data contracts, and v2 path.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm check:quality` ✅
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/page.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/**/*.ts*' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_lib/workspace-*.ts' 'src/hooks/use-realtime-cursors.ts' 'src/lib/supabase/client.ts'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` ✅

- Changes:
  - Added dedicated workspace persistence tables (`organization_workspace_boards`, `organization_workspace_invites`) with UTC timestamps, indexes, triggers, and strict RLS policies.
  - Added typed Supabase schema definitions and registered both tables in `PublicTables`.
  - Reworked workspace server actions to write/read table-backed data first while preserving legacy profile-key fallbacks (`workspace_board_v1`, `workspace_collaboration_v1`) when tables are unavailable.
  - Updated workspace page hydration to load board and invite state from table-backed storage first, then merge with legacy profile data for backward-compatible rendering.
  - Added acceptance tests for storage fallback/merge behavior to prevent regressions during migration rollout.
  - Added v2 storage plan doc for future Codex sessions.

    - Surfaced connection health in workspace collaboration indicator and lightweight cursor-status banner.

  - Reduced competing mousemove work during card drags by suspending local realtime cursor publishing while a workspace node is actively dragging (keeps cursor subscriptions/remote cursors mounted, pauses local broadcast path only).
  - Memoized remaining React Flow config object props (`fitViewOptions`, `defaultEdgeOptions`, `proOptions`) to avoid per-render object churn on the flow surface.
  - Set `nodeDragThreshold={0}` for more immediate drag response.
  - Added a drag-state paint optimization (`.workspace-flow .react-flow__node.dragging [data-workspace-card]`) to remove box-shadow while dragging and reduce paint cost.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas.tsx' 'src/components/realtime-cursors.tsx' 'src/hooks/use-realtime-cursors.ts'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --skipLibCheck true` ✅

## 2026-02-24 — Codex architecture/perf pass 183 (`workspace uncontrolled React Flow migration for drag/zoom responsiveness`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas.tsx`
  - `docs/plans/2026-02-23-organization-workspace-phased-delivery.md`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Migrated `WorkspaceBoardFlowSurface` off the controlled React Flow `useNodesState(...)` drag loop.
  - Workspace canvas now uses an uncontrolled React Flow node model:
    - passes `defaultNodes` to `<ReactFlow />`,
    - stores the `ReactFlowInstance` in a ref,
    - uses imperative `instance.setNodes(...)` only for discrete sync events (board-state changes, snap-on-drop position correction).
  - React Flow now owns live node movement during drag/zoom/pan, while app `boardState` remains the persisted source of truth updated on drag stop / actions.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --skipLibCheck true` ✅

## 2026-02-24 — Codex layout review (`workspace board right rail`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-right-rail.tsx`
  - `src/components/app-shell/components/shell-right-rail.tsx`
- Observations:
  - Elevated nested containers (`RAIL_SECTION_CLASSNAME` slots and stacked `Card`s) double up borders/shadows inside the first `RightRailSlot`, which shrinks the usable space and makes the layout feel visually crowded instead of a single coherent module.
  - The layout controls (preset select, auto-layout, and save button) share a `Card` but the Save action sits outside the grey helper block without consistent spacing, so the primary action floats and loses the visual grouping it needs for clarity.
  - The bottom `Back to dashboard` card is pinned via `RightRailSlot align="bottom"` yet `ShellRightRail`’s scroll container lacks any bottom padding, so the CTA hugs the viewport edge and risks being clipped by the rail’s overflow/scroll chrome (no safe-area inset or breathing room).

## 2026-02-26 — Codex billing hardening pass 263 (`add Stripe checkout price diagnostics for prod price_not_found incidents`)

- Files:
  - `src/lib/billing/stripe-checkout-diagnostics.ts`
  - `src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts`
  - `src/app/(public)/pricing/actions.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Added a shared Stripe checkout diagnostics helper that, on checkout failures, can probe the configured price ID in the selected Stripe runtime and alternate runtime(s) (tester/live) to detect likely mode/account mismatches.
  - Wired diagnostics into the API checkout route error path (`/api/stripe/checkout`) for `price_not_found` failures:
    - logs selected Stripe target/mode
    - logs selected price id
    - logs whether the price exists in the selected runtime vs alternate runtime(s)
    - adds a human-readable hint when the price exists only in the alternate runtime (tester/live routing mismatch signal)
  - Wired the same diagnostics into the public pricing server action checkout error logging for parity.
  - Expanded checkout error logs with non-secret context (`fallbackIsTester`, `audienceIsTester`, `stripeTarget`, `stripeMode`, `selectedPriceId`) to speed up production incident triage using `checkout_debug` references.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/lib/billing/stripe-checkout-diagnostics.ts' 'src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts' 'src/app/(public)/pricing/actions.ts'` ✅

  - `src/app/(dashboard)/_lib/dashboard-layout-state.types.ts`

  - `src/components/app-sidebar.tsx`
  - `src/components/nav-user.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Root cause: `NavUser` rendered `displayName` + `displayEmail`, and `displayName` fell back to the email when the user name was missing, causing email to appear on both lines.
  - Threaded an optional `title` field through dashboard layout state -> app shell -> sidebar -> `NavUser`.
  - Sidebar account card now prefers `name` + `title`; if title is missing, it falls back to email only when it is distinct from the primary line (prevents duplicate email display).
  - Dashboard layout now populates the sidebar user `title` from the resolved org owner/person title when available.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/_lib/dashboard-layout-state.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/_lib/dashboard-layout-state.types.ts' 'src/components/app-shell/types.ts' 'src/components/app-shell/app-shell-inner.tsx' 'src/components/app-sidebar.tsx' 'src/components/nav-user.tsx'` ✅

## 2026-02-26 — Codex account-settings bugfix pass 268 (`stabilize avatar crop/apply flow + harden avatar preview rendering`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/account-settings/account-settings-dialog.tsx`
  - `src/components/account-settings/sections/desktop/profile.tsx`
  - `src/components/account-settings/sections/mobile-sections.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Hardened the avatar crop/apply flow to reduce freeze/re-render issues after clicking `Apply`:
    - validates returned `avatarUrl` response payload
    - closes and resets the crop dialog state before triggering a router refresh
    - cleans up object URLs (`URL.revokeObjectURL`) on replace/close/unmount to avoid leaks and stale crop state
    - wraps `router.refresh()` in `requestAnimationFrame` after successful apply so the nested dialog can close first
  - Hardened account-settings avatar previews (desktop + mobile) for user-uploaded URLs:
    - avatar preview images now render `unoptimized`
    - keyed by `avatarUrl` to force refresh of the rendered image element when the URL changes
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/account-settings/account-settings-dialog.tsx' 'src/components/account-settings/sections/desktop/profile.tsx' 'src/components/account-settings/sections/mobile-sections.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --skipLibCheck true` ✅

## 2026-02-26 — Codex dev/billing pass 269 (`disable react-grab localhost ws spam by default + add avatar upload 500 diagnostics`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/dev/react-grab-loader.tsx`
  - `src/app/api/account/avatar/route.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Disabled `ReactGrabLoader` auto-loading on localhost/dev by default (now opt-in via `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_REACT_GRAB=1`) to stop repeated `ws://localhost:4722` console connection failures when the companion process is not running.
  - Added server-side diagnostics to `/api/account/avatar`:
    - logs user-client upload failure
    - logs fallback admin upload failure
    - returns a `debugToken` in 500 responses for easier correlation
  - This is intended to separate unrelated dev-tool websocket noise from the real avatar upload backend error path.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/dev/react-grab-loader.tsx' 'src/app/api/account/avatar/route.ts'` ✅

## 2026-02-26 — Codex avatar/realtime diagnostics pass 270 (`fix avatar profile-save recursion path + surface debug tokens + reduce dev warnings`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/api/account/avatar/route.ts`
  - `src/components/account-settings/account-settings-dialog.tsx`
  - `src/app/layout.tsx`
  - `src/components/people/org-chart-canvas.tsx`
  - `supabase/migrations/20260226170000_fix_profiles_update_policy_recursion.sql`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Fixed the avatar upload save path to avoid failing when `profiles` self-update RLS recurses:
    - `/api/account/avatar` now attempts `profiles.avatar_url` persistence with the authenticated client first, then falls back to an admin-client `profiles` upsert (after auth is already verified in-route)
    - added targeted logging for the profile-save stage under the existing avatar `debugToken`
  - Improved account-settings avatar upload error handling:
    - when `/api/account/avatar` returns a `debugToken`, the UI now includes it in the thrown/toasted error message (`ref: <token>`) for faster log correlation
  - Added a database migration to fix the underlying `profiles_update` policy recursion:
    - introduces `public.current_profile_role()` and `public.current_profile_is_tester()` security-definer helpers (`row_security = off`)
    - recreates `profiles_update` to use those helpers instead of querying `public.profiles` directly inside the `profiles` policy
  - Reduced a noisy dev warning source by disabling preload for `JetBrains_Mono` in the root layout (preloaded font not used quickly warning)
  - Lifted additional React Flow config objects in the org chart canvas to stable module-level constants (`defaultViewport`, `proOptions`, `defaultEdgeOptions`) to reduce prop-object churn
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/api/account/avatar/route.ts' 'src/components/account-settings/account-settings-dialog.tsx' 'src/app/layout.tsx' 'src/components/people/org-chart-canvas.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --skipLibCheck true` ✅
  - `pnpm test:rls` ✅

## 2026-02-26 — Codex release sweep pass 272 (`apply avatar policy fix migration + restore perf budgets after global shell regression`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/nav-user.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Applied the new local/linked Supabase migrations via `pnpm db:push` (linked project reported up to date), as part of the avatar/profile upload recovery sweep.
  - Re-ran `pnpm check:quality`; all gates passed until `check:perf`, which failed on shared shell bundle budgets:
    - `/dashboard` 1336.7KB (budget ≤ 900KB)
    - `/admin` 1347.1KB (budget ≤ 1000KB)
  - Investigated built manifests/chunks and identified a shared global-shell client bundle regression path:
    - `NavUser` imported `useCurrentUserImage()`, which pulls the browser Supabase client into every dashboard/admin page
    - this brought `@supabase/supabase-js` into the first-load shared bundle
  - Fixed by removing the Supabase session-avatar hook from `NavUser` and relying on the server-provided avatar (`user.avatar`) in the global sidebar account card.
    - account/avatar updates still refresh the shell via `router.refresh()` after successful uploads
  - Rebuilt and re-ran perf budgets; both routes are back under budget.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/nav-user.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false --skipLibCheck true` ✅
  - `pnpm build` ✅
  - `pnpm check:perf` ✅ (`/dashboard: 865.9KB`, `/admin: 876.3KB`)

## 2026-02-26 — Codex dev tooling pass 276 (`React Grab auto-load on any dev host + persistent local disable`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/dev/react-grab-loader.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Fixed React Grab auto-load so it works on any dev host (including LAN IPs like `192.168.x.x`), not just `localhost` / `127.0.0.1`.
  - Added a persistent local disable state:
    - `?reactGrab=0` now stores `"0"` in localStorage (instead of clearing), so dev default-on does not immediately re-enable on the next page.
    - `?reactGrab=1` stores `"1"` and re-enables.
  - Kept env overrides:
    - `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_REACT_GRAB=1` forces on
    - `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_REACT_GRAB=0` forces off

    - `Vault`: categorized document intake with compact category tiles and recent uploads.
    - `Atlas`: minimal location planner supporting online and on-site entries.

  - Added default hidden-card state for new tool cards so workspace launches focused and cards can be toggled in from docks.

## 2026-02-27 — Codex billing reliability pass 297 (`live Stripe checkout/promo verification + tester-routing fix`)

- Files:
  - `src/lib/devtools/audience.ts`
  - `tests/acceptance/devtools-audience.test.ts` (new)
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Ran direct live Stripe verification for production billing readiness:
    - confirmed live runtime key usage (`sk_live_*`) and valid active recurring prices:
      - organization: `$20/mo` (`price_1T2EuGGWpMHCiyVju04mQin0`)
      - operations support: `$58/mo` (`price_1T2EuHGWpMHCiyVjqdV2jsqO`)
    - confirmed both production promo codes are active and valid:
      - `EARLYADOPTER20CAP50` (`max_redemptions: 50`, `times_redeemed: 0`)
      - `EARLYADOPTER20` (uncapped)
    - confirmed live checkout session creation works for organization plan with and without capped promo code; probe sessions were immediately expired.
    - confirmed capped promo does **not** apply to operations-support plan (`coupon_applies_to_nothing`), so the discount remains constrained to the intended tier.
    - confirmed live webhook endpoint is configured and enabled for subscription + checkout events.
  - Investigated production/test routing risk:
    - found users with legacy auth metadata `qa_tester: true` could be routed to tester Stripe despite profile `is_tester = false`.
    - fixed precedence in devtools audience resolution so explicit profile `is_tester` boolean wins over metadata fallback.
  - Added acceptance coverage for the precedence fix in `devtools-audience` tests.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/lib/devtools/audience.ts tests/acceptance/devtools-audience.test.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/acceptance/devtools-audience.test.ts tests/acceptance/pricing.test.ts tests/acceptance/pricing-accelerator-checkout-metadata.test.ts tests/acceptance/billing.test.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm check:quality` ✅

    - cleanup only executes when no dialog/sheet remains open.
- Validation:

## 2026-02-27 — Codex reliability pass 312 (`root-cause sweep for stale interaction locks`)

- Files:
  - `src/lib/ui/interaction-lock-guard.ts` (new)
  - `src/components/app-shell/app-shell-inner.tsx`
  - `src/components/ui/dialog.tsx`
  - `src/components/ui/sheet.tsx`
  - `src/components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx`
  - `src/components/ui/drawer.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Culprit analysis:
  - Remaining stale-lock risk was not limited to `Dialog`/`Sheet`; `AlertDialog` and `Drawer` primitives also lacked lock-release cleanup on unmount.
  - A user-specific sequence (route change/unmount while one of these overlays is open) can leave global body/html interaction locks (`overflow`, `pointer-events`, `data-scroll-locked`) behind.
- Changes:
  - Added shared guard utility to detect active overlays and release stale global interaction locks when none are open.
  - Wired guard into:
    - app shell route/focus/visibility/pageshow recovery,
    - `DialogContent` unmount cleanup,
    - `SheetContent` unmount cleanup,
    - `AlertDialogContent` unmount cleanup,
    - `DrawerContent` unmount cleanup.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/lib/ui/interaction-lock-guard.ts src/components/app-shell/app-shell-inner.tsx src/components/ui/dialog.tsx src/components/ui/sheet.tsx src/components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx src/components/ui/drawer.tsx` ✅

## 2026-02-27 — Codex UI pass 316 (`calendar canvas card clipping fix via node height contract`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - Workspace canvas nodes use explicit width/height from `resolveCardDimensions`.
  - Calendar `sm` node height was fixed at `264px`, which became too short after filled notifications/events/objectives content was added.
- Changes:
  - Increased calendar `sm` node height override from `264` to `360` so the card frame can fully display the populated calendar content without bottom clipping.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout.ts'` ✅

## 2026-02-27 — Codex UI pass 317 (`calendar card clipping removal + dynamic node-height baseline`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-flow-surface.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-card-frame.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-calendar-card-strip.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - Workspace card nodes were rendered with hard `height` in React Flow and card wrappers used strict `h-full` patterns, so added event content in calendar could still be clipped by node/card boundaries.
- Changes:
  - Switched React Flow node sizing from fixed `height` to `minHeight` so cards can expand with content while preserving baseline layout dimensions.
  - Updated workspace card wrappers to use `h-auto` in canvas mode (fullscreen remains `h-full`) to allow content-driven growth.
  - Removed fixed-height wrapper usage in workspace card node host (`h-full` removed).
  - Removed the explicit `Notifications` heading in calendar events panel.
  - Relaxed calendar panel internal flex constraints (`flex-1/min-h-0` sections) so content can grow naturally instead of compressing into a clipped region.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-flow-surface.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-card-frame.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-calendar-card-strip.tsx'` ✅

## 2026-03-01 — Codex implementation pass 47 (`react-flow internals API compatibility fix`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-flow-surface-repair.ts`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-flow-surface.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Removed direct dependency on `instance.updateNodeInternals` in workspace repair path.
  - Replaced repair behavior with a React Flow-safe node refresh (`instance.setNodes((prev) => prev.map((node) => ({ ...node })))`) plus optional `fitView`.
  - Cleaned repair hook signature by removing unused `composedNodes` argument.
  - Updated flow surface callsite to match new hook contract.
  - Purpose: prevent runtime crash `instance.updateNodeInternals is not a function` on environments where the instance method is unavailable.

  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-types.ts`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-copy.ts`

- Changes:
  - Added a dedicated `workspace-accelerator-card` feature module with:
    - typed timeline step contract,
    - pure data normalization/resource parsing + step sequence builder,
    - adaptive size resolver,
    - client controller hook for step navigation and auto-resize,
    - client UI panel with top-right chevron navigation, mini video preview, resources list, status/meta chips, and open-lesson CTA.
  - Added implementation brief documenting architecture, scope, and acceptance in `docs/plans/2026-03-01-workspace-accelerator-card-design.md`.
  - Extended workspace seed contract with optional `acceleratorTimeline`.
  - Added server-side timeline shaping in `my-organization-page-content`:
    - enrich accelerator modules with `module_content` (video/resources), `module_assignments`, and `modules` metadata,
    - normalize resources defensively,
    - build ordered card step sequence.
  - Extended `buildWorkspaceViewSeed` input to carry `acceleratorTimeline`.
  - Replaced `formation-status` card rendering with the new Accelerator card panel while preserving card id (no board-state id migration).
  - Updated card metadata + dock label:
    - title/subtitle now reflect Accelerator scope,
    - dock label for `formation-status` set to `Accelerator`.
  - Updated legacy formation card size overrides to values aligned with the new accelerator card content model:
    - `sm: 252`, `md: 340`.

- Changes:
  - Rebased `/find` to render through `HomeCanvasPreview` directly so it uses the same current homepage shell contract (left rail, top controls, centered canvas card) instead of a separate `PublicHeader` wrapper.
  - Added a dedicated `find` canvas section id to home-canvas config so `/find` can mount as a first-class panel state.
  - Added `findPanel` support in `HomeCanvasPreview` and wired `/find` to inject `PublicMapIndex` into that panel slot.
  - Updated the left-rail `Find` link state to render active styling when the current section is `find`.
  - Locked section-navigation gestures while the `find` panel is active:
    - canvas wheel/touch section switching is disabled for map panel,
    - map touch/wheel interactions are preserved inside the canvas card.
  - Updated `home-canvas-behavior` acceptance coverage for the new `lockNavigationGestures` contract and `find` touch behavior.

## 2026-03-02 — Codex implementation pass 68 (`find route guardrail to prevent shell regression`)

- Files:
  - `scripts/check-route-entry-contract.mjs`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Added a hard route-entry contract for `src/app/(public)/find/page.tsx` to prevent shell regressions.
  - Contract now enforces:
    - required import: `@/components/public/home-canvas-preview`
    - forbidden import: `@/components/public/public-header`
    - required source usage pattern: `<HomeCanvasPreview` in the route file
  - This runs in existing CI/quality gates through `pnpm check:routes` and fails fast if `/find` bypasses the live HomeCanvas shell again.

  - `pnpm check:routes` ✅

## 2026-03-02 — Codex implementation pass 71 (`hard-disable Mapbox events telemetry endpoint`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:

## 2026-03-02 — Codex implementation pass 72 (`fix getter-only EVENTS_URL crash in map init`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Replaced invalid direct assignment to `mapboxgl.config.EVENTS_URL` (getter-only) with a safe descriptor-based override:
    - checks property descriptor for configurability,
    - uses `Object.defineProperty` to set a fixed `null` value when allowed,
    - avoids runtime `TypeError` that was preventing map initialization.
  - Kept `performanceMetricsCollection: false` on map construction.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` ✅

## 2026-03-02 — Codex implementation pass 73 (`fix blank map: Mapbox CSS overriding absolute container positioning`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Root cause: the map container used `absolute inset-0` on the same node Mapbox decorates with `.mapboxgl-map` (which sets `position: relative`), collapsing container height and causing blank/white map rendering.
  - Fixed by splitting wrapper and map target:
    - outer wrapper: `absolute inset-0`
    - inner map target (`ref`): `h-full w-full`
  - Verified runtime dimensions after patch:
    - map container and canvas now both match full surface bounds.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm check:quality` ✅

    - marker sync effect: add/update/remove markers by organization id,

  - Result: clicking heart/save (including auto-opening right saved rail) no longer forces marker avatar images to reload.

## 2026-03-02 — Codex implementation pass 90 (`secure hidden /db-viewer route for non-technical team DB visibility`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/db-viewer/page.tsx`
  - `src/features/internal-db-viewer/**`
  - `src/lib/env.ts`
  - `.env.example`
  - `tests/acceptance/internal-db-viewer.test.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Scaffolded a dedicated feature slice: `internal-db-viewer`.
  - Added hidden route `/db-viewer` (dashboard composition route) that renders the new viewer panel.
  - Implemented strict server-side access control:
    - requires authenticated session,
    - allows access only for `profiles.role = admin` or emails listed in `INTERNAL_DB_VIEWER_ALLOWED_EMAILS`,
    - returns `404` for unauthorized users to keep the route non-discoverable.
  - Implemented read-only table browsing with server-side service-role fetch after authz:
    - table names are validated against known public schema names,
    - only tables in `INTERNAL_DB_VIEWER_TABLES` are queryable,
    - row limits are clamped to safe presets (`25`, `50`, `100`),
    - no arbitrary SQL endpoint was added.
  - Added UI for table selection, row-limit selection, and tabular row rendering with error/empty states.
  - Added env support and docs for team allowlist + table allowlist configuration.
  - Added acceptance tests for parser/guardrail helpers in the feature lib.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/app/'(dashboard)'/db-viewer/page.tsx src/features/internal-db-viewer/index.ts src/features/internal-db-viewer/types.ts src/features/internal-db-viewer/lib/index.ts src/features/internal-db-viewer/server/actions.ts src/features/internal-db-viewer/components/internal-db-viewer-panel.tsx tests/acceptance/internal-db-viewer.test.ts src/lib/env.ts` ✅

## 2026-03-02 — Codex investigation (`/find` marker avatar regression)

- Files:
  - `src/lib/queries/public-map-index.ts`
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index/map-markers.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Findings:
  - Marker avatars are built by `createOrganizationMarkerElement`/`updateOrganizationMarkerElement` using the `logoUrl`/`headerUrl` strings supplied by `fetchPublicMapOrganizations`.
  - If those fields are empty (either because the profile JSON keys were renamed/cleaned up or because whitespace-only values survived the server trim), `syncMarkerAvatarImage` never receives a source and the initials fallback stays visible, matching the recent regression report.
- Next:
  - Consider normalizing the profile assets as part of `fetchPublicMapOrganizations` (trim, guard against `logo_url`/`header_url` variants, return `null` when the value is empty) so that the marker update hook always has an actual URL to feed into `syncMarkerAvatarImage` and the avatar `<img>` shows instead of the fallback.

  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Rebuilt the workspace `Documents` (Vault) card into mode-based surfaces switched from the card overflow menu:
    - `Drop zone`
    - `Search`
    - `Mini viewer`
  - Added Vault mode state wiring through workspace flow node data so canvas and fullscreen card instances stay aligned.
  - Removed stacked “files + mini viewer” sections in Vault and replaced with a single fixed viewport surface per mode (no in-card scrolling), with full-bleed dropzone content and centered icon/title copy.
  - Expanded Vault card height overrides to better support fixed-ratio mode surfaces (`sm`/`md`).
  - Added server route `GET /api/account/workspace-documents-index` that returns recency-sorted items across uploads, policies, roadmap sections, and module notes for in-card search.
  - Implemented search-mode behavior to prioritize newest documents first and route selections into mini-viewer mode.
  - Added download support in `/organization/documents` row actions for uploads and policy files.
  - Converted upload/download row controls to icon buttons with hover tooltips.

## 2026-03-03 — Codex investigation pass (`workspace accelerator click repro + warning profiling`)

- Continuation target: `docs/agent/HANDOFF.md` → executed "First Steps for Next Agent" against the reported accelerator freeze/warning issue.
- Reproduced flow with browser automation (seeded launch account) and captured per-click outcomes:
  - Workspace dock "Accelerator" click:
    - resulting URL: `/organization/workspace`
    - accelerator card visibility: present (`formation-status` visible)
    - hidden cards signal: no hidden workspace cards observed in dock state
    - console errors/warnings during click: none
  - Sidebar "Accelerator" click:
    - resulting URL: `/accelerator`
    - console errors/warnings during click: none
  - Accelerator card CTA click:
    - resulting URL: `/accelerator/class/electives/module/4`
    - console errors/warnings during click: none
  - Accelerator card open-step action click:
    - resulting URL: `/organization/workspace`
    - accelerator card visibility: present (`formation-status` visible)
    - console errors/warnings during click: none
- Aggregate runtime warning evidence (same session):
  - React Flow dev warning still emitted:
    - `[React Flow] ... nodeTypes or edgeTypes object ... #002`
  - No page-level runtime errors observed in repro flow.
- Transition profiling (`/organization/workspace` -> sidebar `Accelerator`) in same account/session:
  - first profiled click:
    - observed click-to-route change: `~1.65s`
    - navigation timing duration: `~2.97s`
    - slow request: `/accelerator?_rsc=...` fetch (~510ms)
  - second profiled click:
    - observed click-to-route change: `~2.19s`
    - navigation timing duration: `~4.60s`
    - slow request: `/accelerator?_rsc=...` fetch (~1.11s)
- Isolation conclusion from live repro:
  - Behavior is reproducible without browser extensions/devtools plugins in headless automation, so extension interference is unlikely to be the primary cause in this environment.
  - Transition delay is primarily associated with route/data loading (`_rsc` fetch + navigation timing), not a hard crash.
  - React Flow `#002` warning remains workspace-route scoped and still appears twice per workspace load in dev.
- Additional validation probe:
  - Captured warning phases show `#002` warnings only during workspace route loads (`workspace-first`, `workspace-second`), not during `/accelerator` navigation.

  - Added acceptance coverage for malformed hidden payload that leaves only one visible card.

## 2026-03-04 — Codex implementation pass 197 (`disable auto React Grab injection in local dev`)

- Files:
  - `src/components/dev/react-grab-loader.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Removed auto-enable behavior for React Grab in local/dev hosts.
  - React Grab now loads only when explicitly enabled via one of:
    - `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_REACT_GRAB=1`,
    - URL query `?reactGrab=1` / `?react-grab=1`,
    - persisted opt-in `localStorage['coachhouse:react-grab-enabled']='1'`.
  - This prevents unsolicited `client.global.js` runtime (`ws://localhost:4722`) and its reconnect/message overhead from affecting workspace interaction performance.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint 'src/components/dev/react-grab-loader.tsx'` ✅

- Files:

- Changes:

  - Added explicit accelerator UI state (`acceleratorUi.stepOpen`, `acceleratorUi.lastStepId`) with legacy hydration fallback from accelerator progress.

    - dock accelerator toggle closes both accelerator card + step node on hide,

  - Removed interaction-time sanitize effect loop from canvas; sanitize/normalize now runs at hydration boundary.
  - Replaced accelerator visibility fit side effect loop with one-shot smart viewport command (`maybeFitAcceleratorIfOffscreen`) triggered only on explicit accelerator reopen requests.
  - Reduced default runtime telemetry surface toward action-level diagnostics by removing render/composition log streams from active flow path.

- Files:

  - `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/hooks/use-workspace-accelerator-card-controller.ts`

- Changes:
  - Fixed accelerator controller rehydration loop conditions:
    - `initialCompletedStepIds` normalization now preserves array identity when already valid,
    - localStorage hydration now runs once per `storageKey` and uses idempotent setters.
  - Reduced canvas-wide render churn caused by accelerator runtime callbacks:
    - runtime callback props are now attached only to the accelerator node data,
    - node memo comparator now gates accelerator-specific comparisons to `cardId === "accelerator"` (and vault-specific comparisons to `cardId === "vault"`).
  - Hardened accelerator board-state sync:
    - accelerator progress sync now uses ordered list equality checks and no-op early returns,
    - sync is no longer blocked by `allowEditing`, preventing stale external-state feedback loops.
  - Fixed assignment autosave/submit signaling and deduplication:
    - `onSubmit` types now accept async return values,
    - submission handler now returns a real async lifecycle and tracks true in-flight state,
    - silent autosave no longer forces `router.refresh()` on every save,
    - autosave now dedupes by payload signature and skips re-submitting identical in-flight/already-submitted payloads.

  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/components/workspace-canvas-surface-v2.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Reworked drag-frame handling to React Flow’s local node-state path using `useNodesState(...)` and `onNodesChange`.
    - This removes frame-by-frame drag dependence on external position synchronization and avoids controlled-position jitter.
  - Added explicit node `dragHandle` selector (`.workspace-card-drag-handle`) so drag initiation is constrained to the card header grip path.
  - Kept persistence on `onNodeDragStop` only, with a value-equality guard against redundant board-state writes.
  - Removed leftover store reset effect from the surface component to avoid stale, unnecessary dispatch logic.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/components/workspace-canvas-surface-v2.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-visibility-reducer.test.ts` ✅

## 2026-03-05 — Codex implementation pass 235 (`fix accelerator dock toggle contract in canvas handler`)

- Files:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas-docks.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - The board-level toggle handler had a special `isDockAcceleratorClick` branch that converted accelerator dock clicks into "focus only" behavior when accelerator was already visible.
  - That path bypassed normal hide/show reduction, so accelerator could not be toggled off while other cards could.
- Changes:
  - Removed the accelerator-only dock-focus early-return branch from `buildToggleCardVisibilityHandler`.
  - Accelerator now follows the same reducer toggle contract as other cards (`dock_toggle_card`).
  - Updated dock action label logic so accelerator shows `Hide`/`Show` instead of `Focus`/`Show`.
  - Preserved existing focus-request behavior only on show transitions (no change to hide path behavior).
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-canvas-docks.tsx'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-board-visibility-reducer.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-accelerator-step-node-visibility.test.ts` ✅

    - kept step node lifecycle local to accelerator runtime (`onRuntimeChange` + `onRuntimeActionsChange`) and not coupled to global card visibility lock behavior.

## 2026-03-05 — Codex implementation pass 238 (`fix accelerator controller max-depth loop from steps identity churn`)

- Files:
  - `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/hooks/use-workspace-accelerator-card-controller.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - The controller sync effects depended directly on `steps` array identity and performed state-sync writes (`setCurrentIndex`/`setCompletedStepIds`).
  - When upstream renders recreated `steps` references, those effects could re-run repeatedly and cascade into React `Maximum update depth exceeded`.
- Changes:
  - Added stable derived step metadata in-controller:
    - `stepIds`, `stepIdsSignature`, `stepIdToIndex`, `validStepIds`.
  - Replaced step-sync effects to use stable signatures/lookup values instead of raw `steps` identity.
  - Made current-step sync idempotent via guarded setter (`previous === nextIndex ? previous : nextIndex`).
  - Updated completed/clamp sync effects to use the same stable step-id derivations.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint 'src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/hooks/use-workspace-accelerator-card-controller.ts'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-accelerator-step-node-visibility.test.ts` ✅

## 2026-03-05 — Codex implementation pass 239 (`fix Next 16 cookie-write crash in server Supabase client during RSC render`)

- Files:
  - `src/lib/supabase/server.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - `createSupabaseServerClient` used deprecated `cookies.get/set/remove` adapter and attempted `cookieStore.set(...)` during server-component render.
  - Next.js 16 throws in that context: `Cookies can only be modified in a Server Action or Route Handler`.
- Changes:
  - Migrated server client cookie adapter to `getAll` / `setAll` (preferred `@supabase/ssr` API).
  - Added guarded handling for read-only cookie writes in RSC render:
    - if Next throws the specific read-only cookies error, writes are safely ignored,
    - non-cookie-context errors still rethrow.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint src/lib/supabase/server.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-view-seed.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts tests/acceptance/my-organization-calendar-view.test.ts` ✅

## 2026-03-05 — Codex implementation pass 240 (`stabilize accelerator badge/state against repeated external sync reapply`)

- Files:
  - `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/hooks/use-workspace-accelerator-card-controller.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Root cause:
  - External state sync effects (`initialCurrentStepId` / `initialCompletedStepIds`) could be re-applied repeatedly when parent inputs churned by reference, causing visible badge/status thrash.
- Changes:
  - Added semantic sync-key guards to apply external sync only once per meaningful signature:
    - `initialCurrentStepId + stepIdsSignature`
    - `initialCompletedStepIdsSignature + stepIdsSignature`
  - Preserved idempotent setters so no-op syncs do not schedule state changes.
  - Added explicit empty-initial-completed handling with no-op guard.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm eslint 'src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/hooks/use-workspace-accelerator-card-controller.ts'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` ✅
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-accelerator-step-node-visibility.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts` ✅

- Changes:
  - Added `WorkspaceAutoLayoutMode` (`hub` | `timeline`) to board state and persistence normalization.
  - Added deterministic dual autolayout engine (`workspace-board-auto-layout-modes.ts`) and wired layout mode actions from right rail controls.
  - Added right-rail layout controls to apply `Hub` (documents-centered) and `Timeline` (left-to-right lane) layouts.
  - Expanded workspace card size tokens to include `lg` and tuned communications/vault sizing overrides.
  - Upgraded v2 card contract with explicit AI-safe structure metadata:
    - `allowedSizes`, `defaultSize`
    - `scrollPolicy`
    - `layoutRoles` (hub/timeline)
    - typed `ports`
  - Refactored connection contract to source port definitions from the shared card contract.
  - Enforced contract-aware size normalization in v2 node data helpers.
  - Fixed dropzone text clipping by removing forced nowrap behavior in dropzone button/content defaults.
  - Hardened calendar preview panel to a single intentional scroll region and improved compact content overflow behavior.
  - Reduced communications compact-preview dead-space by tightening compact content stack behavior.
  - Reduced accelerator controller hydration churn by signature-based initialization guard (`hasInitialCompletedFromInput`).
  - Added acceptance coverage for:
    - default board `autoLayoutMode`
    - `hub` layout behavior
    - `timeline` layout behavior
    - card-contract size/port/layout-role invariants.

  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/contracts/workspace-card-contract.ts`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/runtime/workspace-canvas-camera-controller.ts`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-journey.ts`
  - `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/components/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.tsx`
  - `tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts`
  - `tests/acceptance/workspace-board-journey.test.ts`
  - `tests/acceptance/workspace-view-seed.test.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Reworked workspace journey derivation so the board now reads organization-profile completeness, teammate count, uploaded workspace documents, and accelerator progress from the server-built seed instead of guessing from card state.
  - Replaced the old radial Hub autolayout with a deterministic dashboard composition centered on Documents/Vault, and changed Timeline UI copy to `Linear` while keeping the persisted `timeline` enum for compatibility.
  - Changed the default workspace board mode for new/missing state to the guided Linear rail and reordered the primary lane to `Organization -> Documents -> Accelerator -> Brand Kit -> Fundraising -> Calendar -> Communications`.
  - Updated default workspace connections to reflect the guided progression (`organization -> vault -> accelerator -> execution cards`).
  - Moved the in-canvas guidance surface into the Accelerator card itself with a compact guide banner, stage checklist, CTA routing, and target-card emphasis.

- Changes:
  - Replaced the old communications-only mini heatmap with a shared workspace activity heatmap that aggregates calendar events, board meetings, accelerator progress, publishing activity, and future-ready donation records.
  - Removed the legacy Scheduler stat fragments from the Communications card, including the `1/3 live` and `Mar 2, 9:07 PM` values.
  - Added hover and tap day breakdowns so each cell can show the date, totals, and activity list for that day.
  - Added explicit calendar event typing so board meetings, deadlines, milestones, and general meetings can map into the shared activity feed instead of relying on title inference.
  - Kept the existing communications overlay activity state only as an immediate UI overlay and merged it into the new shared activity feed at render time.

## 2026-03-07 - `/find` public org visibility + marker gating
- Scope:
  - `src/lib/queries/public-map-index.ts`
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index/helpers.ts`
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index/organization-list.tsx`
  - `src/components/public/public-map-index/organization-detail.tsx`
  - `tests/acceptance/public-map-index-query.test.ts`
  - `tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Removed the hard requirement that public organizations have `location_lat/location_lng` before they can appear in `/find`.
  - Kept map markers gated on valid org coordinates, so public orgs without an address now stay discoverable in the `/find` list/details but do not attempt marker rendering.
  - Updated the `/find` client selection flow to track the visible org list instead of the marker-only subset, which keeps `/find/[slug]` working for public orgs that do not yet have coordinates.
  - Clarified `/find` copy so the UI explains that publishing controls discovery and addresses only control marker placement.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/lib/queries/public-map-index.ts src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx src/components/public/public-map-index/helpers.ts src/components/public/public-map-index/organization-list.tsx src/components/public/public-map-index/organization-detail.tsx tests/acceptance/public-map-index-query.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm exec vitest run tests/acceptance/public-map-index-query.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm build` ✅

- Scope:

  - Added a shared canvas-node style resolver so the accelerator node keeps a baseline minimum height for layout while omitting a fixed `height`, allowing the React Flow node to grow with accordion/checklist content.
  - Updated both workspace flow builders to render the accelerator node wrapper as intrinsic height instead of `h-full`, and aligned the canvas contract to reflect that the accelerator should not assume an internal scroll region.
  - Added acceptance coverage for the accelerator canvas contract and for the React Flow node builder output so accelerator nodes stay intrinsic-height while other workspace cards remain fixed-height.

  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Switched the workspace accelerator checklist to a single-open, collapsible accordion so module triggers can actually open and close, and so next/previous step navigation forces the newly active module open while closing the last one.
  - Restored a dedicated selected-step summary panel at the bottom of the accelerator card so the currently active step shows its module title, step title, description, and open-step action.
  - Updated checklist copy and layout so module completion reads as `N of M complete`, long module titles can wrap to two lines, and singular top-level module/step counts render correctly.
  - Added helper coverage for completion-label formatting and module-open-state resolution so the checklist sync rules are locked down.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/components/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.tsx src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/components/workspace-accelerator-card-checklist.tsx src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/components/workspace-accelerator-card-current-step-panel.tsx src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/lib/checklist.ts src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/lib/index.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-checklist.test.ts` ✅

- Changes:
  - Fixed the mobile workspace-shortcut hydration mismatch by mounting the Vaul drawer client-side and keeping a stable pre-hydration trigger fallback.
  - Added a workspace org-card fallback that turns legacy comma-separated profile program titles into displayable program cards when structured `programs` rows do not exist yet, and uses that same fallback for the Programs stat count.
  - Kept the multi-program org-card preview behavior, so the workspace organization card now supports multiple visible program cards at once from either structured rows or legacy program titles.
  - Converted the calendar workspace card to the same intrinsic-height canvas path used by the org/accelerator cards, removed the compact-card inner tab scroll region, and raised the calendar baseline min-height so its content can render directly in the card instead of being trapped in a scroll area.

## 2026-03-07 - program budget breakdown drives fundraising target

- Scope:
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/budget.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/feasibility.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/serialize.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/hydrate.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/summary.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/validation-helpers.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/schema.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/step-budget-feasibility.tsx`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/step-review-generate.tsx`
  - `src/components/organization/program-builder-dashboard-card.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node-static-cards.tsx`
  - `tests/acceptance/program-wizard-budget.test.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Added an explicit budget-breakdown helper so Program Wizard derives total budget and fundraising need from the line items instead of treating one manual budget number as the fundraising target.
  - Updated the budget step to collect cost buckets plus already-raised/committed funding, then show read-only totals for program budget, raised/committed funds, and fundraising need.
  - Changed serialization so `goal_cents` now stores the derived fundraising need while the full program budget remains in the wizard snapshot for downstream displays.
  - Updated review/copy surfaces and the program builder dashboard so labels distinguish `Budget` from `Fundraising target`, and aligned the workspace funding labels from `Goal/Target` to `Need`.
  - Updated the seeded `Bright Futures Collective` program (`Future Builders Mentorship Lab`, `b28e0e50-5b4d-43ab-8b55-15e755a65d2c`) so its stored fundraising target reflects the new budget-derived semantics.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/budget.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/feasibility.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/serialize.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/hydrate.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/summary.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/validation-helpers.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/step-budget-feasibility.tsx' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/step-review-generate.tsx' 'src/components/organization/program-builder-dashboard-card.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node-static-cards.tsx' 'tests/acceptance/program-wizard-budget.test.ts'` ✅

  - `pnpm build` ✅

## 2026-03-08 - program wizard budget step uses accelerator table

- Scope:
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/budget.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/schema.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/feasibility.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/serialize.ts`
  - `src/components/programs/program-wizard/helpers/hydrate.ts`

- Changes:
  - Replaced the custom Program Wizard budget inputs with the same `AssignmentBudgetTableField` used in accelerator budget steps so budget entry now follows one shared table pattern.
  - Added row-based `budgetRows` handling to the wizard schema, hydration, serialization, and validation so existing legacy cost-bucket records still load while new edits persist true line items.

  - `src/app/api/account/program-media/route.ts`
  - `tests/acceptance/program-wizard-media.test.ts`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Added a real `Remove image` action to the shared Program Wizard media field so both the program banner and program profile image can be cleared directly from the wizard.
  - In create mode, removing an uploaded program-media image now also calls a new authenticated `DELETE /api/account/program-media` path to clean up the uploaded asset immediately instead of only hiding the preview.
  - In edit mode, removal clears the field in the wizard and relies on the existing autosave/server cleanup path so persisted banner/profile images are removed safely without double-deleting storage objects.
  - Added coverage for recognizing uploaded program-media URLs so client-side cleanup only targets org-owned storage objects.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/lib/storage/program-media.ts' 'src/app/api/account/program-media/route.ts' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard.tsx' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/program-wizard-step-content.tsx' 'src/components/programs/program-wizard/components/step-basic-info.tsx' 'tests/acceptance/program-wizard-media.test.ts'` ✅
  - `pnpm exec vitest run tests/acceptance/program-wizard-media.test.ts` ✅
  - `pnpm build` ✅

- Changes:
  - Moved the initial `intent/org/account` onboarding flow off the dashboard shell dialog path and into a dedicated single-card workspace canvas surface.
  - Reused the existing onboarding step, slug validation, avatar, draft, and submit logic through a shared `OnboardingFlow` component instead of creating a second onboarding implementation.

  - Hid the normal workspace canvas and right rail while initial onboarding is active, then kept the regular workspace available again after completion.
  - Redirected onboarding-locked users from non-workspace dashboard routes into `/workspace?onboarding_flow=1&source=onboarding` and removed the old inline shell onboarding mount.
  - Registered a dedicated React Flow onboarding node type and suppressed empty mobile shortcut chrome when no card shortcuts are available.

  - Applied the same shared handle styling to the special accelerator-step and onboarding-guide nodes.

## 2026-03-09 13:41 EDT - synced workspace card shells with canvas node height contracts

- Scope:
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-card-frame.tsx`
  - `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-organization-card-shell.tsx`
  - `docs/RUNLOG.md`
- Changes:
  - Applied the shared canvas node height contract directly to the visible workspace card shells so the rendered card and the React Flow node wrapper stay aligned.
  - The issue was not the React Flow `NodeWrapper` by itself; the wrapper respected the shared `minHeight`, but the visible card shell did not, leaving an invisible tail below shorter card content.
  - Organization and shared workspace card shells now inherit the same `minHeight`/`height` values used by the canvas node style, while still allowing auto-height cards to grow beyond that baseline when their content expands.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-card-frame.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-organization-card-shell.tsx'` ✅

  - Changed tutorial camera fitting to trigger only on tutorial navigation/open actions, while still fitting the guide plus currently visible tutorial cluster with extra padding for active callouts.

2026-03-12 14:09 EDT - added hCaptcha support to the shared signup form by wiring the official `@hcaptcha/react-hcaptcha` widget into signup-only flows, introducing the optional public `NEXT_PUBLIC_HCAPTCHA_SITE_KEY` env, and passing `options.captchaToken` to `supabase.auth.signUp()` when configured; also added a confirm-password field with zod validation so mismatched passwords are blocked before submit. Login remained unchanged. Validation passed with focused `vitest`, focused `eslint`, and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 14:18 EDT - added Vercel Analytics to the root App Router layout by installing `@vercel/analytics` and mounting `<Analytics />` in `src/app/layout.tsx` so analytics runs across the entire app. Validation passed with focused `eslint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 17:43 EDT - restored the older onboarding-style formation-status card treatment on the public `/find` organization detail sidebar by updating the contained `OrganizationFormationStatusSummary` variant to use the prior rounded card layout with the circular check indicator, while leaving the current onboarding toggle mechanics unchanged. Validation passed with focused `eslint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 17:54 EDT - tightened the public organization detail action-link buttons by reducing their height from `h-20` to `h-16` and shrinking the icon-to-label gap so labels sit closer to the icons without changing the grid or behavior. Validation passed with focused `eslint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 17:58 EDT - made the entire public `/find` organization list card reliably open details by letting the existing full-card overlay button receive clicks through non-interactive content layers, while keeping the favorite heart and `Details` affordance independently clickable; also removed the container chrome from `Details` so it renders as icon + text only. Validation passed with focused `eslint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 18:12 EDT - added hCaptcha handling to the password login form so local and production sign-in work when Supabase captcha is enabled: the login form now renders the shared hCaptcha widget below the password field, blocks submit until it is completed, passes `options.captchaToken` into `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`, and resets the widget after failed attempts. Validation passed with focused `eslint` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-12 18:51 EDT - installed the project-scoped `hcaptcha-public-docs` agent skill at `.agents/skills/hcaptcha-public-docs/SKILL.md` so Codex can read and cite the public hCaptcha LLM markdown docs with local search-index discovery and enterprise handoff guidance.
2026-03-13 10:55 EDT - disabled hCaptcha across the app by adding a default-off `NEXT_PUBLIC_HCAPTCHA_ENABLED` gate that all active auth captcha checks now respect, so login/signup no longer render the widget or send captcha tokens unless that flag is explicitly re-enabled later. The shared widget remains in place for future reactivation, but runtime behavior is now off by default even if a site key is still configured. Validation passed with focused `eslint`, `pnpm exec vitest run tests/acceptance/sign-up-form-schema.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-13 11:33 EDT - isolated a follow-up auth rollback from clean `origin/main` and removed hCaptcha from the active login/signup app surfaces while leaving the shared widget/helper files in the repo for later reactivation. The shipped auth forms now go back to plain Supabase email/password flows with no captcha UI, no captcha submit gating, and no captcha token forwarding in the live app bundle. Validation passed with focused `eslint`, `pnpm exec vitest run tests/acceptance/sign-up-form-schema.test.ts tests/acceptance/auth-recovery.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
2026-03-13 15:43 EDT - implemented the hybrid organization invite/access-request system and internal prototype lab. Added `organization_access_requests` with RLS + schema types, created existing-user invite routing through in-app notifications plus Resend heads-up email, kept tokenized email invites for non-users, added signed-in `/access-requests` review/accept/decline flow, introduced the admin-only `/admin/platform/prototypes` lab with centered invite/email canvases, and added the admin auth-template sync action/button plus shared Coach House email previews for invite/auth templates. Also pushed the two new migrations to the linked Supabase project: `20260313190000_add_organization_access_requests.sql` and `20260313193000_restrict_organization_access_request_updates.sql`.

2026-03-13 16:11 EDT - fixed the notifications bell trigger so the popover opens again. `NotificationsBellButton` had been used under `PopoverTrigger asChild` without forwarding Radix’s injected props/ref, which meant the bell swallowed the trigger handlers and never opened the notifications popover. Converted it to a ref-forwarding wrapper that spreads the trigger props through to the underlying shared `Button`. Validation passed with `pnpm exec eslint src/components/notifications/notifications-menu.tsx` and `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.

2026-03-13 16:44 EDT - refined workspace collaboration invites and reset the prototype lab UI. Temporary invite notifications now get archived when an invite is revoked. The prototype lab now uses a minimal file-tree left rail with one centered prototype canvas at a time instead of the previous stacked card-heavy preview chrome.

2026-03-13 21:26 EDT - implemented a calm guided transition layer for workspace onboarding without changing the tutorial content model. Step changes now use authored layout/camera timing instead of snapping, the welcome guide uses the organization card as its width reference, the guide is draggable from its header on every tutorial step, and dragged positions animate back onto the authored path on next/back. The guide content now crossfades/slides between steps, the welcome card drops its shadow, and the board finally opts into the existing node transform animation hook during tutorial scene changes.

2026-03-13 22:03 EDT - fixed tutorial next/back viewport drift in the workspace canvas. The tutorial scene-fit request now carries a layout key derived from the currently rendered scene nodes, and the camera controller treats that as part of the handled request state. That lets the tutorial re-run its authored fit after the canvas nodes finish reconciling to the new step layout, instead of only fitting once against stale pre-reconcile positions.

2026-03-14 00:57 EDT - stopped the tutorial camera from competing with generic canvas fit/focus effects. While a tutorial scene-fit request is active, the camera controller now skips the initial-fit, layout-fit, accelerator-focus, and card-focus paths, and it ignores duplicate rerenders of the same scene-fit request until the pending request settles. That removes the remaining zoom/recenter thrash caused by multiple camera effects firing against the same onboarding transition.

## 2026-03-14 — Codex session (workspace tutorial shell + restart control)

- Reworked the workspace tutorial card into a composite shell that can render embedded workspace surfaces below the guide copy, keeping the existing guide header/progress styling while stabilizing the presentation area for onboarding steps.
- Replaced the typewriter-style tutorial body updates with step-level copy transitions only, added a persistent presentation key for embedded accelerator/org scenes, and suppressed duplicate live canvas nodes while those same surfaces are rendered inside the tutorial shell.
- Added a dev-only top-left `Restart guide` canvas overlay control plus a full tutorial reset path that reactivates onboarding, clears opened/acknowledged progress, closes the accelerator step node, and persists the reset to workspace onboarding user metadata.
- Added the tutorial restart board-state helper and expanded onboarding-flow acceptance coverage for restarting from a partially completed tutorial.

## 2026-03-14 — Codex session (workspace shortcut open fix + tooltip indicator)

- Fixed the tutorial shortcut presentation handoff by threading `openedTutorialStepIds` into the tutorial shell presentation resolver, so clicking a guided shortcut now swaps the shell from the organization rail to the connected target card on that same step instead of appearing stuck.
- Extracted the shortcut-step presentation decision into a pure helper and added focused regression coverage for accelerator and later shortcut steps.
- Removed the tutorial-only Radix tooltip wrapper from organization shortcut buttons and replaced it with a compact inline arrow indicator, so the guide still points at the target button without opening a floating layer over the card UI.
- Kept the existing tooltip-based tutorial callouts for accelerator navigation, picker, progress, module-close, and team-access surfaces by making `WorkspaceTutorialCallout` support both tooltip and inline-indicator modes.

## 2026-03-14 — Codex session (workspace tutorial dock mask)

- Replaced the tutorial panel’s embedded-card slot with a transparent dashed dock mask for dockable workspace cards, so the live React Flow card can show through the shell instead of a duplicated embedded renderer.
- Made tutorial node chrome pass pointer events through the dock area while keeping the header/copy controls interactive, which lets the live card underneath remain clickable and draggable during the walkthrough.
- Added tutorial dock-target math and snap logic so dockable cards start docked by default, can be dragged out during tutorial mode, and snap back into the slot when dropped near the target area.
- Threaded tutorial-specific draggable card IDs and dock-derived position overrides through initial node building, reconciliation, and drag-stop handling without persisting those temporary positions to the saved workspace layout.

## 2026-03-14 — Codex session (workspace tutorial unscaled canvas measurements)

- Fixed a React Flow zoom-space bug in the tutorial docking flow: node and mask measurements were using `getBoundingClientRect()`, which returns zoom-scaled values and caused the organization card, dashed frame, and guide shell to disagree at non-1x zoom.
- Switched the workspace-card and tutorial-node measurements to unscaled layout dimensions, and normalized the measured mask rect back into canvas-space before using it as the dock target.
- This keeps the tutorial shell height, dashed frame size, and dock target in the same coordinate system so the organization card no longer drifts up-left or overlaps the tutorial copy when the canvas is zoomed.

## 2026-03-15 — Codex session (workspace accelerator shortcut rail focus fix)

- Fixed the organization-card accelerator shortcut so a click now focuses the accelerator card instead of taking the generic visible-child branch that toggled and refocused the parent rail group.
- Kept a legacy recovery path for poisoned hidden-state snapshots: if accelerator is somehow hidden, the shortcut opens it first and then focuses it.
- Added acceptance coverage for both the visible accelerator case and the legacy hidden accelerator case in the shortcut model tests.
- Validation:
  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/shortcuts/workspace-card-shortcut-model.ts' 'tests/acceptance/workspace-card-shortcuts.test.ts'`
  - `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-card-shortcuts.test.ts`

## 2026-03-15 — Codex session (workspace onboarding guide live-card stabilization)

- Replaced the tutorial’s organization/tool dock-mask ownership path with embedded shared `WorkspaceBoardCard` surfaces inside the guide shell, so guided clicks now hit the same live card component instead of a masked canvas node behind the guide.
- Kept the dashed frame as a visual container, but moved the interactive organization/tool card into that frame and suppressed the corresponding canvas node during guided ownership.
- Disabled dragging for the tutorial guide node and removed the disabled-next tooltip layer from the guide header to reduce overlay interference.
- Added persistent canvas viewport controls for zoom in, zoom out, recenter, and reset, all wired to the same React Flow instance used by guided and regular canvas flows.
- Increased same-scene tutorial camera moves from zero-duration to a short animated refocus so step-to-step guided changes can visibly punch in/punch out without a full layout jump.

## 2026-03-15 — Codex session (live browser pass + accelerator shortcut click fix)

- Ran a real authenticated Playwright pass against `http://127.0.0.1:3000/workspace?onboarding_flow=1`, restarted the guide, advanced to step 4, and confirmed the embedded Accelerator shortcut was still failing in the browser even though the model tests passed.
- Traced the failure to `WorkspaceCardShortcutButton`: the button was calling `stopPropagation()` from `onClickCapture` on the same target that owned `onClick`, which prevented the shortcut press handler from firing in the live guide card.
- Removed the `onClickCapture` propagation trap while keeping the pointer-down propagation guards needed for React Flow interaction containment.
- Removed the temporary shortcut debug logs added during browser diagnosis.
- Re-ran the live browser flow and verified the step-4 Accelerator button now swaps the guide content from the organization card to the accelerator card inside the tutorial container.

## 2026-03-15 22:06 EDT - workspace guide stability/performance pass

- Refactored the workspace guide runtime around stable presentation families instead of raw step indices, so tutorial scene signatures now dedupe same-family copy changes instead of refitting the camera on nearly every step.
- Stabilized the guide shell with shared family-level shell specs for overview, tool, and accelerator scenes; the tutorial node now uses fixed width/height per family and no longer repositions from measured guide height.
- Removed extra tutorial render churn by:
  - dropping the duplicate `setNodes(reconcile(...))` pass from `workspace-canvas-surface-v2.tsx`,
  - removing attached-guide height measurement state from the tutorial runtime,
  - short-circuiting the tutorial node `ResizeObserver` when no measurements are needed,
  - making scene-fit handling idempotent per request key instead of refiring on `layoutKey` churn.
- Converted the tutorial panel into a fixed-height flex shell with internal scroll regions so embedded card content no longer resizes the guide shell step-by-step.
- Updated shortcut-opened scene layout resolution so a revealed target adopts its target scene layout immediately instead of staying anchored to the old overview layout.
- Removed the competing focus-camera request from tutorial-highlighted shortcut presses and changed shortcut-step completion to advance immediately into the next tutorial step once the user clicks the required shortcut.
- Added and updated regression coverage for stable scene-family signatures, shortcut-step scene adoption, and the no-focus tutorial shortcut path.

## 2026-03-15 23:28 EDT - workspace guide center-lock motion fix

- Reworked tutorial scene signatures to follow stable stage families (`welcome`, `overview`, `tool`, `accelerator`) instead of active card ids, which stops same-family steps from reissuing camera moves.
- Center-locked the tutorial node to a single authored stage anchor per breakpoint and switched the camera to derive its viewport center from the guide shell itself, so shell size changes grow from the center instead of shifting from the top-left.
- Simplified guided camera framing to be tutorial-shell-first: scene fit requests now only wait on the tutorial node, not the surrounding canvas cards, which removes pan-heavy refits when guided cards are embedded inside the shell.
- Unified accelerator module steps onto the same accelerator-family stage so deeper accelerator onboarding no longer zooms/pans to a separate authored scene.

- Added regression coverage that locks guide-center stability across welcome -> overview and accelerator -> accelerator-module transitions.
