## 2026-03-19 14:28 EDT - add deterministic React Grab ownership metadata and collapse tutorial surface ownership

- Added a shared React Grab ownership helper so semantic owners and linked portal surfaces now carry explicit owner/component/source metadata instead of hand-authored `data-react-grab-*` attributes.
- Updated the React Grab loader to resolve linked surfaces by explicit owner id, added a dev-only trace path, and kept legacy link-id compatibility during the migration window.
- Migrated the current high-frequency tutorial surfaces to the new ownership model:

- Added repository docs for pasted-component ownership workflow and updated workspace presentation/quality contracts to reference the new React Grab execution rules.

- Unified the guided checklist step callout bubble with the same inverse tutorial tooltip surface used by the shortcut rail and picker callouts.
  - `workspace-accelerator-card-checklist.tsx` now passes `WORKSPACE_TUTORIAL_INVERSE_TOOLTIP_CLASSNAME` into the targeted `WorkspaceTutorialCallout`, so the highlighted lesson-step bubble uses the shared inverse tooltip token instead of falling back to base `TooltipContent` popover styling.

- Consolidated the first live workspace card-layout ownership pass so shell height mode and canvas shell sizing stop being redefined in the frame, organization shell, and node wrapper layers.
  - `workspace-board-layout-config.ts` is now the shared owner for:
    - intrinsic-height card ids via `isWorkspaceCardAutoHeight(...)`
    - height mode class selection via `resolveWorkspaceCardHeightModeClassName(...)`
    - canvas shell style via `resolveWorkspaceCardCanvasShellStyle(...)`
    - canvas shell class composition via `resolveWorkspaceCardCanvasShellClassName(...)`
  - rewired `workspace-board-node-class-name.ts` to delegate to the layout-config helpers instead of maintaining a second auto-height card set
  - rewired `workspace-board-card-frame.tsx` to use the shared canvas shell helpers instead of locally deciding fixed-height vs intrinsic-height treatment
  - rewired `workspace-board-organization-card-shell.tsx` to use the same shared canvas shell helpers as the generic card frame
  - rewired `workspace-board-node.tsx` to derive its root `h-auto` / `h-full` mode from the shared layout helper instead of another inline branch
  - exported the new helpers from `workspace-board-layout.ts` and added focused acceptance coverage in `workspace-board-layout.test.ts`

  - `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout-config.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node-class-name.ts' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-card-frame.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-organization-card-shell.tsx' 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node.tsx' 'tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts' 'tests/acceptance/workspace-board-handle-styles.test.ts'`

- Consolidated the active viewport execution path so manual recenter/reset and automatic camera requests share one executor, and removed the dead legacy viewport helpers that were implying a parallel system.
  - expanded `workspace-canvas-viewport-command.ts` with:
    - `fit-nodes` command support for accelerator-branch fits
    - a typed `WorkspaceCanvasViewportExecutionResult`
    - shared execution return metadata so camera logs no longer need their own duplicate `fitView` path
  - rewired `workspace-canvas-camera-controller.ts` so:
    - initial fit
    - visible-node recovery fit
    - explicit layout fit
    - scene-fit execution
    - accelerator branch focus
    - explicit card focus
    all route through `executeWorkspaceCanvasViewportCommand(...)`
  - decomposed the camera controller into internal effect hooks so the consolidated controller stays under the repo function-size limit without reintroducing behavioral forks
  - removed the unused legacy files:
    - `workspace-board-flow-surface-repair.ts`
    - `workspace-board-flow-surface-fit-view.ts`
    - `workspace-board-viewport-commands.ts`
  - added shared executor coverage for accelerator-branch fits in `workspace-canvas-viewport-command.test.ts`

- Consolidated workspace tutorial/accelerator motion staging into one shared motion spec so panel shell, skeleton, overlays, and local step-content swaps stop carrying their own timing tables.
  - added `src/lib/workspace-canvas/motion-spec.ts` as the shared owner for:
    - scene transition timing (`same-family`, `family-change`, `welcome-handoff`, `accelerator-entry`, `accelerator-preview-exit`)
    - presentation handoff delays
    - shared staged easing
    - per-stage motion specs for `presentation-frame`, `presentation-skeleton`, `bottom-fade`, `copy-shell`, `copy-heading`, `copy-body`, and `content-swap`
  - rewired `workspace-canvas-motion-grammar.ts` to delegate its scene timing contract to the shared motion spec instead of owning a second timing table
  - simplified `workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel-motion.ts` so it only resolves semantic presentation presets and transition keys; timing now comes from the shared motion spec
  - updated `workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel.tsx` and `workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel-skeleton.tsx` to consume the shared stage motion helpers for:
    - copy rail fade
    - heading/body staged reveal
    - framed presentation entry/exit
    - skeleton entry/exit
    - bottom gradient/fade entry
  - updated `workspace-accelerator-step-node-card.tsx` to replace its local hard-coded content swap animation with the shared `content-swap` motion stage, so the embedded accelerator preview no longer carries its own timing/easing island
  - added focused motion coverage in `tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-motion-spec.test.ts`

2026-03-20 14:36 EDT - removed the broader “keep previously opened tools visible” tutorial rule so later guide steps stop leaking old cards onto the live canvas after refresh. The underlying contract lived in `src/features/workspace-canvas-tutorial/lib/index.ts`: `resolveWorkspaceCanvasTutorialVisibleCardIds(...)` was unioning every previously opened shortcut target into the current step’s visible-card set, which is why opening Accelerator early could make it reappear behind Calendar, Programs, and later steps after hydration. The visibility rule is now single-scene: unopened shortcut steps show the prompt scene (`organization-overview`), and opened shortcut steps show only the current target card. Updated `tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial.test.ts` accordingly and verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-onboarding-flow.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 14:48 EDT - delayed all always-open workspace tutorial callouts until their anchors have finished settling so they no longer pop in at the wrong coordinates and then jump. In `src/components/workspace/workspace-tutorial-callout.tsx`, tutorial callout bubbles now stay visually hidden for a shared settle delay (`420ms`, reduced to `120ms` under reduced motion), keyed to the specific callout target. The bubble still mounts so Radix can track the anchor while the target animates, but users do not see it until the anchor has stabilized. This automatically covers shortcut-button callouts, accelerator internal callouts, and the Team Access guide callout because they all route through `WorkspaceTutorialCallout`. Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-tutorial-callout.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-header-picker.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-checklist.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-card-shortcuts.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial.test.ts`.

2026-03-20 15:52 EDT - changed tutorial completion to land on one deterministic post-guide rest layout instead of preserving the pre-guide `timeline` mode. The screenshot the user reported was still consistent with the old behavior because `buildCompletedWorkspaceTutorialBoardState(...)` in `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-onboarding-flow.ts` was keeping `boardState.autoLayoutMode`, which left the completed board in the wide timeline rail layout and made the new camera handoff look like “no change.” Completion now forces `autoLayoutMode: "dagre-tree"` and rebuilds the visible rest-state nodes in that mode before the explicit completion-exit request runs, so the workspace should land on the compact connected graph rather than the stretched timeline arrangement. Updated `tests/acceptance/workspace-board-canvas-helpers.test.ts` accordingly and re-verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-onboarding-flow.ts' 'tests/acceptance/workspace-board-canvas-helpers.test.ts'` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-board-canvas-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-onboarding-flow.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-viewport-command.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 16:04 EDT - fixed the module-preview/tutorial content-mode shell wrappers so accelerator module preview and tool steps stop inheriting fill-mode height behavior. The issue was not the `body-shell` itself but `src/features/workspace-canvas-tutorial/components/workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel.tsx`: the tutorial panel root `Card`, `CardContent`, and `body-shell` were still rendering with `h-full`/`flex-1` even when the presentation surface had `heightMode: "content"`. That meant the module-preview scene kept measuring its already-stretched `960px` shell instead of its intrinsic rendered height, which is why the guide showed a large dead area below the dashed frame and an over-zoomed feeling around the component. The panel wrappers now switch between fill and content behavior based on `presentationSurface.heightMode`, allowing content-mode scenes to collapse to intrinsic height and feed that smaller shell height back through the existing measurement path. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/features/workspace-canvas-tutorial/components/workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel.tsx'` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.

2026-03-20 16:23 EDT - moved the internal workspace tutorial restart control to the bottom-left canvas corner and hardened its visibility so it only renders in local development editing mode. The button is still rendered from `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/components/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-view.tsx`, but `workspace-canvas-surface-v2.tsx` now delegates the gate to `workspace-canvas-surface-v2-debug-controls.ts` via `shouldShowWorkspaceCanvasInternalTutorialRestart(...)`, which returns true only for `allowEditing && !presentationMode && NODE_ENV === "development"`. This is intentionally stricter than the previous `NODE_ENV !== "production"` check because there is no existing super-admin signal threaded into this client surface, so the safest contract is local-dev only rather than pretending we have RBAC we do not. Added focused coverage in `tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-debug-controls.test.ts` and verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-debug-controls.test.ts`.

2026-03-20 17:18 EDT - increased the shared Roadmap card shell height so the embedded navigator can show the full roadmap list without truncating inside the workspace canvas. The real owner was `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout-config.ts`, where the new `roadmap` card had inherited an undersized fixed-height shell (`sm: 432`, `md: 456`, `lg: 540`) that was appropriate for the old Documents/Vault card but too short for the roadmap TOC. Raised the Roadmap shell contract to `sm: 560`, `md: 600`, `lg: 680` and updated the fixed-shell regression in `tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts`. Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 17:21 EDT - converted the new workspace Roadmap card from a fixed-height shell to the same intrinsic-height behavior used by the accelerator/organization cards. The issue was not just “make it taller”; the Roadmap card was still outside `AUTO_HEIGHT_CARD_IDS` in `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-layout-config.ts`, so the board kept forcing a fixed shell and the inner roadmap wrapper in `workspace-board-node-tool-card-roadmap.tsx` was compensating with its own scroll region. That made the card rigid when the roadmap was open and prevented it from collapsing cleanly when the TOC header chevron closed. Roadmap is now an auto-height card in the shared layout contract, its `CardContent` path in `workspace-board-node-card.tsx` no longer forces `flex-1`, and the roadmap card wrapper no longer adds an inner `overflow-auto` region. This lets the full roadmap list determine the live card height and lets the shell shrink again when the TOC is collapsed, preserving a real bottom gap instead of dead scroll slack. Updated `tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts` to assert the new `h-auto`/no-fixed-shell behavior and re-verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-board-layout.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-card-shortcuts.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 17:25 EDT - fixed a maximum update-depth loop in the accelerator tutorial presentation path. The problem was `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/components/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial-node-state.tsx` still wiring `onMeasuredHeightChange` for the regular `accelerator` tutorial family, even though that scene uses an authored shell contract rather than adaptive shell measurement. That let the rendered accelerator overview feed measured card-content height back into tutorial presentation state on every render, which could churn around the progress-rail/tooltip trigger subtree and surface as the React max-update-depth error from `AcceleratorProgressRail`. Tightened `shouldWorkspaceTutorialMeasurePresentationContentHeight(...)` in `workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial-shell-height.ts` so only the fixed centered card families that actually need presentation-content measurement (`overview` and `map`) report it; `accelerator`, `tool`, and `accelerator-module` no longer feed content height back through that path. Updated `tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-shell-height.test.ts` and verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-shell-height.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-tutorial-policy.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 17:35 EDT - tightened the accelerator runtime bridge so the tutorial presenter stops re-emitting semantically identical accelerator snapshots through the workspace card tree. The remaining max-update-depth risk was not in `AcceleratorProgressRail` itself; it was the runtime callback path. `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/lib/runtime-snapshot.ts` now owns the shared accelerator runtime snapshot equality contract, `WorkspaceAcceleratorCardPanel` uses that equality before emitting `onRuntimeChange`, and `workspace-board-node-card.tsx` now uses the same guard before forwarding accelerator runtime changes back up into the workspace canvas. `workspace-canvas-runtime-snapshot.ts` was reduced to a thin re-export so the canvas runtime and accelerator feature use one equality definition instead of drifting. Added `tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-runtime-snapshot.test.ts` and verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-runtime-snapshot.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-tutorial-policy.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 17:48 EDT - stopped the embedded accelerator intro/picker tutorial scenes from mutating live accelerator workspace state. The remaining accelerator tutorial crash was still happening on the noninteractive overview/picker scenes because the tutorial-presented accelerator card was wired through the same live `onAcceleratorStateChange` / runtime callback bridge as the real workspace card, even though those scenes are display-only and do not need to feed state back into the canvas. `workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial-presentation.tsx` now overrides the embedded accelerator callbacks to no-op on `accelerator` and `accelerator-picker`, while still keeping full runtime tracking on `accelerator-first-module` and `accelerator-close-module` where the guide actually depends on current step/module viewer state. The tracking rule now lives in `workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial-presentation-state.ts` via `shouldWorkspaceTutorialTrackEmbeddedAcceleratorRuntime(...)`, and `tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts` now locks that behavior in. Re-verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-runtime-snapshot.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 19:31 EDT - fixed two remaining workspace guide regressions in the embedded presentation path. First, `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-canvas-v2/components/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-help-overlay.tsx` was reading `localStorage` during initial render, which caused an SSR/client hydration mismatch when the canvas help tip had already been dismissed on the client. The help overlay now renders with a stable server/client default and applies the persisted dismissal state after mount. Second, the embedded accelerator tutorial card was still behaving too much like the live workspace card even on display-only scenes: `workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial-presentation.tsx` now forces tutorial-rendered cards into `presentationMode`, and `workspace-board-node-card.tsx` now disables accelerator storage hydration, progress propagation, size syncing, and local runtime header-bridge callbacks for embedded accelerator tutorial steps unless the step explicitly requires runtime tracking (`accelerator-first-module` or `accelerator-close-module`). This removes the last remaining local `WorkspaceBoardCard` feedback path that could still churn around the accelerator progress rail in overview/picker scenes after the earlier outer-callback no-op change. Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-help-overlay.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 20:13 EDT - narrowed the remaining accelerator tutorial crash to the progress-rail tooltip subtree and added an embedded-tutorial-safe fallback. The crash stack repeatedly terminated at `AcceleratorProgressRail -> TooltipTrigger -> Button`, so instead of changing the live accelerator card again, `src/components/accelerator/accelerator-progress-rail.tsx` now supports a non-tooltip checkpoint rendering mode and `workspace-accelerator-card-progress-strip.tsx` / `workspace-accelerator-card-panel-support.tsx` / `workspace-accelerator-card-panel.tsx` now disable the Fundable/Verified milestone tooltips only when the accelerator is rendered inside tutorial interaction policy scenes. Live accelerator cards still render the full milestone tooltips; tutorial-embedded accelerator scenes now render inert checkpoint chips so the guide no longer mounts the tooltip/button subtree that keeps appearing in the max-update-depth stack. Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/accelerator-progress-rail.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts`.
2026-03-20 20:18 EDT - removed a redundant lesson-group state sync that was still causing a max-update-depth loop in the embedded accelerator tutorial path. The concrete runtime stack pointed to `useWorkspaceAcceleratorLessonGroupState` in `src/features/workspace-accelerator-card/components/workspace-accelerator-card-panel-lesson-groups.ts`, where local `lessonGroupFilter` state was being force-synced from `tutorialInteractionPolicy.allowedClassGroupKey` on every render. That sync was unnecessary because `selectedLessonGroupKey` already derives from `tutorialInteractionPolicy.allowedClassGroupKey` with higher precedence than local state. Deleted the effect entirely so tutorial policy remains a pure derived override instead of a state feedback path. Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts`.

Verified with focused `eslint` plus `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-tutorial-presentation.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-canvas-surface-v2-tutorial.test.ts`.

2026-03-23 18:29 EDT - fixed first-load public map marker disappearance and list shrink behavior after clustering rollout. Root cause: clustered-source effects in `src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts` were keyed only to stable refs and did not reliably rerun when `map.on("load")` completed, so source/layers/markers could remain uninitialized; additionally the list in `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx` was still passing `appliedBounds` into `filterPublicMapOrganizations(...)`, collapsing search results to viewport-in-bounds entries after the first camera sync. Fixes: added `mapLoadVersion` state in `public-map-index.tsx` incremented on map load and threaded into clustered marker hook to force source/render effects at the correct lifecycle edge; added hidden hit layers (`public-map-organizations-cluster-hit`, `public-map-organizations-point-hit`) tied to the clustered source so source query/render paths stay active; switched list filtering to all organizations (`appliedBounds: null`) so `/find` sidebar always shows the full matching set. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx' 'src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts' 'src/components/public/public-map-index/map-view-helpers.ts'`, `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-index-map-view-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-markers.test.ts`, and `pnpm build`.
2026-03-23 18:41 EDT - improved public map sidebar list scrolling polish in `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar.tsx`. Added `scroll-smooth` behavior to the search-results scroll container and implemented scroll-aware top/bottom fade overlays inside the `SidebarGroup` list surface. Fade visibility now updates on scroll and resize via `syncListFadeState()` + `ResizeObserver`, with opacity transitions so list boundaries feel softer without adding new surfaces or changing data flow. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar.tsx'` and `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts`.
2026-03-23 18:50 EDT - fixed public map marker highlight color and cluster expansion behavior after user QA. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/map-markers.ts`, selected marker avatar/label borders now use neutral white borders (removing group-accent green outline) and marker buttons explicitly suppress tap-highlight focus chrome via `-webkit-tap-highlight-color`. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts`, removed strict `isSourceLoaded(...)` gating so markers continue updating during pan/partial tile load, and updated cluster click behavior to prefer `getClusterLeaves(...) + fitBounds(...)` (with current map padding) so clicking a cluster reveals the cluster’s member spread instead of jumping into a single point. Kept `getClusterExpansionZoom(...)` as fallback. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/public/public-map-index/map-markers.ts' 'src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts'`, `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-index-map-view-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-markers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts`, and `pnpm build`.
2026-03-23 18:54 EDT - fixed SSR/client boundary regression for React Grab debug helpers causing `/` and other server-rendered routes to crash with `Attempted to call buildReactGrabDebugSurfaceRecord() from the server`. Root cause was `src/components/dev/react-grab-debug-surface.ts` being marked `"use client"` while the helper is imported by server-compatible primitives (`card`, `button`, `select`, `tooltip`). Removed the client directive so the pure helper functions are callable in both server and client module graphs; client-only behavior remains runtime-gated by `typeof window !== "undefined"` inside registry functions. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/dev/react-grab-debug-surface.ts' 'src/components/ui/card.tsx' 'src/components/ui/button.tsx' 'src/components/ui/select.tsx' 'src/components/ui/tooltip.tsx'` and full `pnpm build` (Next.js 16.1.6 Turbopack).
2026-03-23 19:09 EDT - tuned public-map clustering aggressiveness and cluster-click expansion behavior after UX feedback that map remained cluster-only. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/map-view-helpers.ts`, reduced cluster strength (`PUBLIC_MAP_CLUSTER_RADIUS` 28, `PUBLIC_MAP_CLUSTER_MAX_ZOOM` 9) so single markers appear earlier during zoom. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts`, cluster click now prioritizes `getClusterExpansionZoom(...)` with an additional zoom boost (`+0.85`, bounded against current zoom) so expansion breaks clusters faster; retained bounded `getClusterLeaves(...)` fit-bounds as a secondary refinement. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint 'src/components/public/public-map-index/map-view-helpers.ts' 'src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts'`, `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-index-map-view-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-markers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts`, and full `pnpm build`.

2026-03-27 15:04 EDT - replaced the incorrect clustered-source bootstrap with initial-only rendered-layer bootstrap listeners. 2026-03-27 15:14 EDT - corrected the public-map cluster runtime to follow Mapbox’s wrapped-copy and rectangular-projection behavior more closely. Root cause for the remaining bad declustering was twofold: `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx` still initialized the public map with `projection: "globe"`, and `src/components/public/public-map-index/public-map-cluster-runtime.ts` allowed wrapped longitudes from `queryRenderedFeatures(...)` to flow directly into marker state and camera targets. That meant a cluster could render visually in the right place while its stored coordinates pointed at an adjacent world copy, causing click-to-decluster and child-cluster reconciliation to land over water or otherwise drift off the visible aggregate. Fixed by moving the public map back to `mercator`, disabling rendered world copies via `setRenderWorldCopies(false)`, adding `normalizeCoordinatesForMap(...)` / `resolveFeatureCoordinatesForMap(...)` to canonicalize rendered feature coordinates against the current map center before they are used by markers or camera transitions, updating `coalesceClusterReconcileFeatures(...)` to choose a logical cluster/org copy by visible screen position with a canonical-longitude tie-breaker instead of prior geographic coordinates, and updating `resolveClusterClickTarget(...)` plus `src/components/public/public-map-index/unclustered-display-coordinates.ts` to use the same canonicalized coordinates. Added acceptance coverage in `tests/acceptance/public-map-cluster-runtime.test.ts` for wrapped-longitude normalization, screen-position-based copy selection, and click-target resolution. Verified with `pnpm eslint src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx src/components/public/public-map-index/public-map-cluster-runtime.ts src/components/public/public-map-index/use-public-map-clustered-markers.ts src/components/public/public-map-index/unclustered-display-coordinates.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-cluster-runtime.test.ts` and `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-cluster-runtime.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-cluster-leaves.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-overlap-expansion.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-markers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-map-view-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-helpers.test.ts`.

Extracted baseline layer/source upsert logic into `src/components/public/public-map-index/map-layer-sync.ts`, where visible cluster circle/count layers, point layers, and selected-point halo/core layers are now configured and updated directly from the map source instead of via HTML marker mirroring. Extracted wrapped-coordinate canonicalization into `src/components/public/public-map-index/map-coordinate-normalization.ts` to keep the cluster runtime under repo thresholds and make camera/selection math reusable across the cluster runtime, layer hook, and duplicate-coordinate display helpers. In `src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`, switched the list/search pipeline to an indexed model via `src/components/public/public-map-index/search-index.ts`; the page now memoizes a `PublicMapSearchIndex`, filters by ranked org ids, and only then maps back to `PublicMapOrganization` objects for the list/details surfaces. Added acceptance coverage in `tests/acceptance/public-map-search-index.test.ts` for indexed narrative/program matching and online-only behavior, and expanded `tests/acceptance/public-map-cluster-runtime.test.ts` to lock the new visible-layer and selection-filter contract. 2026-04-02 17:27 EDT - restored a reliable high-to-mid collapse path for the `/find` bottom sheet by adding an opt-in interactive Vaul handle only for the public map drawer. 2026-04-02 18:23 EDT - refactored the `/find` mobile bottom sheet to stop reusing the desktop sidebar shell inside Vaul. `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar.tsx` now renders two distinct shells: the existing `SidebarProvider`/`Sidebar` rail for desktop widths, and a dedicated drawer body for mobile that uses a single native scroll container, `data-vaul-no-drag` on scrollable regions, and `handleOnly` with an explicit top handle for snap control. This removes the prior hybrid stack of `Sidebar` + `ScrollArea` + Vaul inside the mobile path, which was the main structural cause of the unreliable top-snap collapse behavior. Verified with `pnpm exec eslint src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar.tsx src/components/ui/drawer.tsx src/components/public/public-map-index.tsx`, `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/public-map-surface-helpers.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-sidebar-snap-points.test.ts tests/acceptance/public-map-index-map-view-helpers.test.ts`, and `pnpm build`. 2026-04-02 18:31 EDT - corrected the public-map drawer gesture contract after the shell split. 2026-04-09 00:03 EDT - reproduced the live `/workspace` accelerator issue with a fresh seeded full account and isolated the broken embedded step-open path. A signed-in browser repro against the running dev server showed the dock `Accelerator` shortcut only triggers a camera focus request and does not hide cards, but clicking a checklist step inside the embedded accelerator card (`workspace-accelerator-checklist:ded5d852-1444-40c4-a62f-d2f83a93ce69:video`) fired the DOM click without opening a step node or committing navigation, while the canvas remounted and re-emitted React Flow `#002`. Instrumentation confirmed `WorkspaceAcceleratorCardPanel.handleStepSelect()` and `WorkspaceBoardCard.handleAcceleratorRequestOpenStep()` both ran and computed the correct `/workspace/accelerator?...` href, but `router.push()` never produced a `pushState` / `replaceState` transition from inside the embedded React Flow card. Updated `src/app/(dashboard)/my-organization/_components/workspace-board/workspace-board-node-card.tsx` so workspace accelerator step-open and paywall-open actions now use `window.location.assign(...)` for those embedded card clicks, which restores reliable navigation out of `/workspace` into `/workspace/accelerator?...` in both dev and production-runtime browser repros. Revalidated with focused eslint on the touched workspace/accelerator files, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test:acceptance tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-card-panel.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-board-accelerator-step-node-visibility.test.ts tests/acceptance/workspace-accelerator-step-node-card.test.ts`, a direct browser repro against the dev server, and a second direct browser repro against a built app served on port `3011`. The React Flow `#002` warning still reproduces on the dev workspace canvas, but it did not appear in the production-runtime repro, so the remaining warning currently looks dev/HMR-specific rather than a production navigation blocker.

After that, ran a fresh production `pnpm build`, re-measured the built client-reference manifests, and confirmed the current route sizes are `/(dashboard)/workspace/page` `3699.7KB`, `/(dashboard)/workspace/accelerator/page` `3699.7KB`, `/(dashboard)/my-organization/page` `2489.7KB`, `/(dashboard)/organization/page` `2489.7KB`, `/(dashboard)/organization/workspace/page` `2489.7KB`, `/(community)/community/page` `646.5KB`, and `/(admin)/admin/page` `968.6KB`. Finally reran the full `pnpm check:quality` gate unrestricted so the live Supabase RLS stage could resolve over the network; it passed end to end, including `pnpm lint`, all structure/boundary guardrails, `pnpm test:snapshots`, `pnpm test:acceptance`, `pnpm test:rls`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test:visual`, and `pnpm check:perf`. Formal ship status at the end of this pass: the dirty tree is quality-gate green and technically pushable, but `/workspace` remains substantially heavier than the currently budgeted `/community` and `/admin` routes, so the next optimization pass should keep targeting accelerator/workspace chunking even though it is no longer a release blocker under the current perf contract.

2026-04-15 01:57 EDT - corrected the remaining public-map rail card constraint bug by moving the right gutter off the Radix viewport and onto a real inner content wrapper. The root cause was that `src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx` only exposed `viewportClassName`, so the rail search panel in `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar-panels.tsx` was trying to create the right-side breathing room by padding the `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport` itself (`pr-*` on the viewport), which does not reliably define the scroll content box for a full-width card list. `ScrollArea` now supports `contentClassName`, rendering an explicit `data-slot="scroll-area-content"` wrapper when needed; the public-map rail uses that wrapper for `pb-4 pr-3`, while the parent section (`data-public-map-sidebar-section="rail-organizations-shell"`) owns the shared `px-3` inset. That makes the parent container actually constrain the list on the right instead of letting cards visually run to the edge. Updated `tests/acceptance/public-map-sidebar-layout.test.ts` to lock the new shell/content contract, then revalidated with focused eslint, focused public-map acceptance, and full `pnpm check:quality` (green: `175 passed | 1 skipped`, RLS passed, production build passed, visual regression passed, perf budgets passed).
2026-04-15 02:24 EDT - fixed the public-map rail overflow that was still visible on the live `/find` page by constraining the Radix `ScrollArea` viewport’s internal content wrapper instead of only styling the list children. Live Playwright measurements against `http://127.0.0.1:3000/find` showed the bug clearly: the rail shell was `389px` wide and the scroll viewport `365px`, but the first organization card was still rendering at `396.4px`, so the list was wider than the actual viewport. The root cause was Radix’s internal viewport child (`min-width: 100%; display: table`), which continued to size the content to max-content width. `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar-panels.tsx` now applies a public-map-specific `viewportClassName` override (`[&>div]:!block [&>div]:!min-w-0 [&>div]:!w-full [&>div]:!max-w-full`) so that internal wrapper behaves like a constrained block box, while `src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx`, `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar-panels.tsx`, and `src/components/public/public-map-index/organization-list.tsx` keep the rail content/list/cards on `w-full min-w-0 max-w-full` with a real `pr-4` trailing gutter. Updated `tests/acceptance/public-map-sidebar-layout.test.ts` to lock the viewport override and width contract. Revalidated with focused eslint, focused public-map acceptance, and a second live Playwright measurement showing the first card back inside the viewport (`349px` card width inside a `365px` scroll viewport, `28px` shell right gap, `16px` content right gap).
2026-04-15 02:36 EDT - equalized the public-map rail list card side insets after the overflow fix left the cards visually heavier on the right. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/sidebar-panels.tsx`, the rail organizations `ScrollArea` now uses balanced inner padding (`contentClassName="px-1 pb-4"`) instead of the old right-only gutter, so the cards keep the same inset on both sides while preserving a small buffer away from the scroll viewport edge. Updated `tests/acceptance/public-map-sidebar-layout.test.ts` for the balanced content wrapper class and revalidated with focused eslint, focused public-map acceptance, and a live Playwright measurement against `http://127.0.0.1:3000/find` showing exact symmetry (`shell_left_gap: 16`, `shell_right_gap: 16`, `content_left_gap: 4`, `content_right_gap: 4`).
2026-04-15 14:09 EDT - manually repaired the live public-map data for real production organizations that had published addresses but still had null `location_lat` / `location_lng`. Queried production `organizations` via Supabase service-role access and confirmed `8` public orgs total with `4` initially missing coordinates; one of those rows was `location_type="online"` and is expected to remain off the physical map. Geocoded and directly patched the three in-person production rows for `Brushworks Community Arts Initiative`, `PLAN4SUCCESS`, and `Defy Ventures`, then rechecked production and confirmed only the online-only org remains without coordinates (`content-range: 0-0/1`). Also confirmed the deployed backfill route `/api/internal/cron/organization-geocoding` is currently returning `500` because `INTERNAL_CRON_SECRET` / `CRON_SECRET` is not configured in production, so future retroactive repairs still need that env fix even though the immediate live map data is now corrected.
2026-04-15 14:40 EDT - tightened public-map marker anchoring on `/find` so the overlay markers track the map continuously during pan/zoom instead of visibly trailing and then snapping into place. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/public-map-marker-overlay.tsx`, the overlay no longer uses the old 32ms timeout/throttle path; it now refreshes off the Mapbox `render` loop, keeps the final reconciliation hooks for `moveend` / `resize` / `idle` / `style.load` / source updates, and uses `flushSync` while the map is actively moving so React commits stay in the same frame as the map draw. In `src/components/public/public-map-index/public-map-marker-overlay-model.ts`, projected marker coordinates are no longer rounded to integers, which removes the extra pixel-stepping during motion. Revalidated with focused eslint plus targeted public-map acceptance (`tests/acceptance/public-map-marker-overlay-buttons.test.ts`, `tests/acceptance/find-map.test.ts`) (green).
2026-04-15 15:33 EDT - fixed the paid builder onboarding dead-end that could trap subscribed users on `/onboarding` after checkout. The root cause was a mismatch between the shell redirect target and the pricing-step recovery logic: onboarding-locked builder users get forced back to `/onboarding?source=onboarding`, but `src/lib/onboarding/pricing-return.ts` only auto-submitted the post-signup pricing step when the URL source was exactly `source=onboarding_pricing`. That left paid users stuck on the pricing screen with a green `Current plan` badge and, in some cases, a stale `checkout_error` banner, while `/workspace` kept redirecting them back into onboarding. The recovery helper now auto-submits any paid `post_signup_access` onboarding pricing step for either onboarding source (`onboarding` or `onboarding_pricing`) and ignores stale checkout error/cancel query params once the resolved builder tier is already paid. Added the incident brief `docs/briefs/2026-04-15-paid-onboarding-stuck-report.md` plus focused acceptance coverage in `tests/acceptance/onboarding-pricing-return.test.ts`, and revalidated with focused eslint plus onboarding acceptance (`onboarding-pricing-return`, `onboarding`, `onboarding-actions`, `app-shell-onboarding-redirect`) (green).
2026-04-15 15:38 EDT - fixed the public-map overlay race introduced by the render-loop marker refresh. `src/components/public/public-map-index/public-map-marker-overlay-model.ts` now uses the existing map-style guards before querying cluster features: it verifies both the organization source and `public-map-organizations-cluster` layer exist, and it returns an empty cluster set instead of calling `map.queryRenderedFeatures()` while the style is still rebuilding. The cluster query is also wrapped defensively so transient style swaps no longer surface `The layer 'public-map-organizations-cluster' does not exist in the map's style` errors in the console. Added focused regression coverage in `tests/acceptance/public-map-marker-overlay-model.test.ts` for both the missing-layer and mid-transition query-throw cases, then revalidated with focused eslint plus targeted public-map acceptance (`public-map-marker-overlay-model`, `public-map-marker-overlay-buttons`, `find-map`) (green).
2026-04-15 15:50 EDT - added an explicit manual paid-onboarding escape hatch so subscribed builder users do not have to rely solely on the pricing auto-submit effect. In `src/components/onboarding/onboarding-dialog/components/pricing-step.tsx`, the current paid builder plan card now renders an enabled `Continue to workspace` submit button during `post_signup_access` onboarding instead of a disabled `Builder access active` badge-only button. `src/components/onboarding/onboarding-dialog/components/step-footer.tsx` also now uses the clearer final-action label `Enter workspace` for that exact paid pricing step, and `onboarding-dialog-content.tsx` passes the flow mode/submitting state through to both surfaces. Added focused regression coverage in `tests/acceptance/onboarding-pricing-step.test.ts` and revalidated with focused eslint plus onboarding acceptance (`onboarding-pricing-step`, `onboarding-pricing-return`, `onboarding`, `onboarding-actions`) (green).
